summary: Reporter wants to apply gamma correction to an SKBitmap using SKMaskFilter.CreateGamma, but MaskFilter has no effect when used with DrawBitmap; they are asking for the correct approach.

codeSnippets: using (var skPaint = new SKPaint()) { using (var maskFilter = SKMaskFilter.CreateGamma(Gamma)) { skPaint.MaskFilter = maskFilter; using (var skCanvas = new SKCanvas(bitmap)) { skCanvas.DrawBitmap(bitmap, new SKRect(0, 0, bitmap.Width, bitmap.Height), skPaint); } } }
stepsToReproduce: Draw an SKBitmap onto itself via SKCanvas.DrawBitmap — observe no visible change

versionAnalysis.relevanceReason: SKMaskFilter.CreateGamma and DrawBitmap behavior have not changed; the by-design limitation applies to all versions.

analysis.summary: SKMaskFilter operates on alpha masks produced during path/text rendering and has no effect when drawing bitmaps via DrawBitmap. To apply gamma correction to bitmap pixel data the reporter should use SKColorFilter.CreateTable() with a power-function lookup table instead.
analysis.rationale: The reporter filed this as a bug but provided no expected or actual behavior. The real question is 'how do I apply gamma to a bitmap?' — SKMaskFilter is not designed for bitmap pixel processing; it targets alpha masks generated by path/shape/text rendering. The correct tool is SKColorFilter.CreateTable() or SKColorFilter.CreateSrgbToLinearGamma/CreateLinearToSrgbGamma. This is by-design behavior, not a defect.

keySignals.text: SKMaskFilter doesn't work for both blur and gamma
keySignals.interpretation: Reporter is confused about which filter type to use for bitmap drawing; MaskFilter is not designed for bitmaps.

keySignals.text: I figured out that blur works via SKImageFilter, but there is no Gamma.
keySignals.interpretation: Reporter already knows blur requires SKImageFilter; they now need the equivalent route for gamma — which is SKColorFilter.

keySignals.text: Expected Behavior: _No response_ / Actual Behavior: _No response_
keySignals.interpretation: No broken behavior described; this is a usage/how-to question.

codeInvestigation.finding: CreateGamma delegates to sk_maskfilter_new_gamma — produces an alpha-mask table filter. Skia applies MaskFilter only when rendering a path/text mask, not during DrawBitmap, so this call has no visible effect on bitmap draws.

SKColorFilter.CreateTable(byte[] table) and CreateTable(tableA, tableR, tableG, tableB) apply a per-channel LUT to every pixel in a DrawBitmap call — the correct mechanism for custom gamma correction on bitmaps.
SKColorFilter.CreateSrgbToLinearGamma() and CreateLinearToSrgbGamma() provide built-in sRGB gamma transfer functions, useful for standard gamma 2.2 correction without a custom table.

workarounds: Use SKColorFilter.CreateTable(gammaTable) with a 256-entry power-function LUT for custom gamma on bitmaps
Use SKColorFilter.CreateSrgbToLinearGamma() or CreateLinearToSrgbGamma() for standard sRGB gamma transfer

resolution.hypothesis: SKMaskFilter is not the right tool for bitmaps; SKColorFilter is the correct SkiaSharp API for per-pixel gamma correction.

proposals.title: Apply gamma via SKColorFilter.CreateTable
proposals.description: Build a 256-entry byte lookup table using a power function (value/255)^gamma and assign it as ColorFilter on the SKPaint used in DrawBitmap.

proposals.title: Use built-in sRGB gamma filters
proposals.description: For standard sRGB ↔ linear gamma, use the built-in SKColorFilter.CreateSrgbToLinearGamma() or CreateLinearToSrgbGamma() without building a custom table.

resolution.recommendedReason: Supports any custom gamma value; the LUT approach exactly mirrors what SKMaskFilter.CreateGamma does but at the pixel level rather than the mask level.

actionability.reason: SKMaskFilter not applying to DrawBitmap is by-design Skia behavior; the workaround using SKColorFilter.CreateTable is well-established and can be documented in the response.

actions.description: Correct label from type/bug to type/question and add area and platform labels
Explain why SKMaskFilter doesn't affect bitmaps and provide SKColorFilter workaround
Close as not a bug — by-design limitation of SKMaskFilter
